radio_sub: Browse and play radio stations from RadioBrowser (50k+ stations, open source)
radio_no_favorites: No favorites yet — search and star stations
spotify_toast_empty_body: Paste a Spotify track / album / playlist URL first.
spotify_toast_no_tracks: No tracks returned — playlist may be empty or region-restricted.
playlist_sub: Curated lists of local audio + streaming URLs. Export as M3U to play on your phone or smartwatch (via phone sync).
playlist_select: Select a playlist or create one
playlist_empty_hint: Add local audio files (MP3/FLAC/M4A) or streaming URLs. Export as M3U to copy onto your phone — most music apps (and watch sync) read M3U natively.
radio_identify_no_match: No match — try when a song is clearly playing
radio_acoustid_unconfigured: Configure AcoustID key in Settings to use identify
rss_empty_hint: Add a feed using the + button above — RSS 2.0, Atom, iTunes podcast feeds AND YouTube channels (paste any youtube.com/@handle or /channel/UC… URL).
downloads_no_path_body: No file path saved for this entry.
downloads_empty: No downloads yet in this session.
settings_update_uptodate: You are on the latest version
settings_show_logs_help: Display the live diagnostic log under Media, Live, Radio, etc. Useful for troubleshooting; hidden by default to keep the UI clean.
settings_folder_unwritable_body: FLUX cannot write to "{path}". Reason: {err}. Pick a folder you own.
settings_delete_source_confirm: Delete source "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
rss_discover_fail_body: The page does not expose an RSS or Atom feed that FLUX could resolve. Paste the direct .xml / .rss feed URL instead.
rss_discover_multi_body: Using {feed}. {extra} other feed(s) were declared on the page.
xtract_sub: Local media manipulation: extract audio, convert, trim, extract subtitles. No internet required.
xtract_ffmpeg_missing: ⚠ ffmpeg not found in PATH — XTRACT requires ffmpeg
xtract_card_audio_help: Strip the audio track and re-encode to your preferred format.
xtract_card_convert_help: Repackage or re-encode to another container/codec.
xtract_card_trim_help: Cut a section, optionally apply fades, and convert to another format. Drag the markers on the waveform or type times.
xtract_card_subs_help: Save the first embedded subtitle track as a .srt file.
xtract_card_frame_help: Save a single frame at a given timestamp as PNG/JPG.
xtract_card_concat_help: Join the loaded file with one or more others of the same format.
xtract_card_meta_help: Dump container tags + stream info to a .json sidecar.
xtract_card_normalize_help: EBU R128 loudness normalisation (loudnorm filter). Targets -14 LUFS.
xtract_no_card_applies: No tools apply to this file type.
spotify_sub: Paste a Spotify track, album or playlist URL — FLUX resolves track titles and downloads matching audio from YouTube.
spotify_url_ph: Paste Spotify playlist / album / track URL…
spotify_err_invalid: Not a recognised Spotify URL. Expected: track / album / playlist link from open.spotify.com
spotify_err_fetch: Failed to load Spotify resource (network or page changed)
spotify_legal_note: Note: matches YouTube searches by title — no Spotify-protected audio is downloaded. Use only for tracks you own or are licensed to download.
spotify_legal_warning: FLUX does NOT download Spotify-protected audio. It reads public track metadata, then searches YouTube and downloads matching audio via yt-dlp. Use only for tracks you legitimately have access to (your own library, free content, or where you hold a Spotify subscription). You are responsible for compliance with the copyright law of your country.
settings_acoustid_help: AcoustID already works with FLUX's built-in key. To use your own (e.g. to avoid the shared rate limit), register a free Application below (the /applications page — NOT the "user submission key" at /api-key), then paste the client key above.
settings_acoustid_invalid_body: Settings not saved: {reason}. Fix the key or switch to Shazamio.
settings_recognition_help: Backend for the song-identify button: Shazam (no key, broad catalogue) or AcoustID (your free API key — Chromaprint + MusicBrainz).
settings_acoustid_invalid_alert: AcoustID is selected but the key is not valid ({reason}). Reverting to Shazamio (default, no key required).
settings_fpcalc_missing: fpcalc missing — rebuild FLUX to install
tag_sub: Read and edit audio file tags (MP3 full support · FLAC/M4A/OGG read-only for now)
tag_drop_zone: Drop audio files here, or click to browse
tag_select_file: Select a file from the list to edit its tags
tag_bulk_no_fields: Fill at least one field on the form before applying to selected files.
tag_bulk_no_edits: No edits to propagate — change a field on the form first, then apply.
tag_bulk_no_baseline: Select a file first, then edit fields, then apply.
tag_bulk_no_mp3: Selected files are not writable (MP3 only).
tag_apply_bulk_icon: Apply form fields to selected files
tag_apply_bulk_icon_count: Apply form fields to {n} selected file(s)
tag_save_unsupported: Save not yet supported for this format (MP3 only)
tag_parse_failed: Filename does not match the pattern
tag_need_query: Need Title or Artist (try "Tag from filename" first or fill manually)
live_sub: Capture live broadcasts and ongoing streaming sessions
live_not_live_warn: This URL is not a live stream. Use the Media tab for regular videos.
torrent_sub: Search across enabled sources — results sorted by seeders
torrent_no_sources: No sources enabled. Enable at least one in Settings.
media_sub: Download audio and video via yt-dlp from any supported platform
media_url_ph: Paste media URL (Vimeo, SoundCloud, direct file, …)
custom_source_body: You are adding a source that is not part of the FLUX defaults (YTS, Nyaa, 1337x, TPB). Content from custom sources has not been reviewed and is entirely your responsibility. Make sure you have the right to download what you find there.
first_download_tip: ⓘ FLUX is for legal use only. Make sure you have the right to download this content.
queue_sub: Batch download — items processed by your concurrency setting
musiclist_intro: Paste one track per line (or load a TXT/CSV). Items are added to the queue and searched on YouTube at download time.
musiclist_placeholder:
Daft Punk - Get Lucky
Adele - Hello
…
musiclist_empty: Paste at least one line, or load a file.
tbatch_intro: Paste one search per line (or load a TXT/CSV). FLUX searches every enabled torrent site for each line and pre-selects the most-seeded result. Review the picks, then add them to the queue.
tbatch_no_queries: Add at least one search line.
queue_send_setup_body: Turn on "Send torrents to client", fill in your client (e.g. qBittorrent) and save.
home_sub: Fetch · Load · Use · eXtract — your open-source media workbench.
home_p1_body: FLUX is a desktop media manager that bundles best-of-breed open-source tools (yt-dlp, ffmpeg, Chromaprint, WaveSurfer) behind a single coherent UI. It lets you fetch media from public sources, organise it locally, and convert or edit it — without juggling command lines or third-party websites.
home_p2_body: Paste any media URL (YouTube, podcast feeds, RSS, internet radio, public Spotify tracks for metadata-only lookup) and FLUX resolves the best playable stream via yt-dlp. RSS feeds support auto-update; podcasts can be played in-app or downloaded as MP3/MP4.
home_p3_body: Build playlists from local files or streaming URLs and export them as M3U for phones or smartwatches. Queue dozens of downloads at once and run them sequentially, or import an existing CSV / TXT list from the Queue tab.
home_p4_body: The Audio Editor reads ID3v2 / FLAC / M4A metadata, looks up missing fields on MusicBrainz, fetches cover art automatically, and writes the result back. Auto-tag fires on every successful audio download — finished files arrive with title, artist, album and artwork already populated.
home_p5_body: Trim a section of any audio or video file with a draggable waveform region, apply optional fade-in / fade-out, and convert to another container — all in one click. ffmpeg does the heavy lifting; lossless stream-copy when the format does not change, re-encode only when needed.
home_p6_title: Use it in line with your country's laws
home_p6_body: FLUX never bypasses DRM and never circumvents access controls. You are responsible for the content you choose to download, store, or distribute. Use it for your own legitimate library: personal recordings, public-domain works, content you have a licence for, or material whose owners have explicitly authorised redistribution.
queue_import_wait_check: Wait until all URLs are verified before adding them to the queue.
queue_empty: Queue is empty — add items from Torrent or Media tabs
rss_sub: Follow any RSS or Atom feed — news, podcasts, public domain content
rss_select: Select a feed to browse its items
rss_no_items: No items found in this feed.
rss_delete_feed_confirm: Delete feed "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
settings_sub: Configure paths, sources, language, theme, downloads and profiles
profile_overwrite_body: A profile named "{name}" already exists and will be overwritten.
profile_delete_last: You can't delete the only profile.
export_full_body:
A full backup includes EVERYTHING in your configuration — local folder paths, sync profiles, playlists, and all credentials (qBittorrent / SABnzbd / IRC SASL / SOCKS proxy passwords, API keys, server tokens).

Keep this file private — it is for your own backup or for moving FLUX to another computer. Do NOT share it. To share your settings, use "Export (shareable)" instead.
settings_speed_limit_help: Caps the download rate per file. 0 disables the cap.
settings_sendto_enabled: Forward magnets to an external client
settings_sendnzb_enabled: Forward .nzb files to an external client
nzb_sub: Forward .nzb files to your SABnzbd or NZBGet server.
nzb_target_unconfigured: NZB forwarding not configured — see Settings → Integrations
irc_sub: Connect to IRC networks and receive files via XDCC bots.
irc_server_tab_hint: Server tab — pick a channel or PM tab to chat
irc_msg_ph: Type a message and press Enter…
irc_sasl_enabled_help: Authenticate with NickServ during the connect handshake
settings_socks_enabled: Route all FLUX traffic through a SOCKS5 proxy
settings_socks_help: Routes all FLUX traffic (IRC, yt-dlp downloads, RSS, MusicBrainz, Spotify, etc.) through the proxy. Examples: 127.0.0.1:9050 for Tor, or a SOCKS5 endpoint from your VPN provider.
settings_mp4_compat_help: Prefer H.264 video + AAC audio inside MP4. Plays in QuickTime, iMovie, iPhone Photos, default Windows player. OFF = smaller file (VP9/AV1) but Mac default players can't read it.
settings_modules_help: FLUX is a modular platform. Each module bundles a related set of features and its own external binaries. Toggle a module off to hide its tabs and slim the UI; required modules stay on. Changes apply after restart.
settings_modules_fetch_all_help: Download all the binaries this module needs
settings_modules_restart_msg: Module changes take effect after restart.
binfetch_confirm_body: FLUX needs to download: {list}. Total ~{size} MB. Continue?
nav_bin_download_title: Download the components this section needs
dlview_sub: This section needs a few open-source tools before it can run. They download once and are kept for next time.
tos_accept_check: I have read and accept the Terms of Use and the Privacy Policy.
tos_accept_1341: Under arts. 1341–1342 of the Italian Civil Code, I specifically approve the following clauses: 1, 3, 4, 6, 7 and 10.
tos_courtesy_note: Courtesy translation — the legally binding version is the Italian one. In case of conflict, the Italian text prevails.
tos_scroll_hint: Scroll to the bottom to enable
tos_section_1_b: By using FLUX (the "Software"), you confirm that you have read, understood, and agree to be legally bound by these Terms. If you do not accept these terms, you must not install or use the Software.
tos_section_2_b: The Software is provided exclusively for lawful purposes. You may only download content that you own, have purchased, or have explicit authorisation for. Permitted uses include: public domain works, Creative Commons licensed material, open-source software, Linux ISO images, and content for which you hold a valid licence. FLUX does NOT bypass any technical protection measure (DRM such as Widevine, FairPlay, or PlayReady). Circumventing DRM is illegal in the EU (Directive 2001/29/EC art. 6) and in the USA (DMCA §1201) — and is explicitly out of scope for this Software.
tos_section_3_b: Downloading, copying, distributing, or reproducing copyrighted material without authorisation is illegal under applicable law. It is your sole responsibility to verify compliance with copyright laws in your jurisdiction.
tos_section_4_b: The authors of FLUX disclaim all liability arising from use or misuse of the Software. The Software is provided "AS IS" without warranty. Any illegal activity is carried out at your own risk and sole legal responsibility.
tos_section_5_b: FLUX does not endorse, promote, or facilitate piracy. The torrent, media, RSS, and other distribution channels are intended solely for legitimate use cases.
tos_section_6_b: The Software interfaces with third-party services that the user enables explicitly: torrent search APIs (YTS, Nyaa, The Pirate Bay), the yt-dlp binary, RadioBrowser API for radio stations, MusicBrainz / Cover Art Archive / LRCLIB for music metadata, and AcoustID (opt-in, requires a free API key) for song recognition. Any RSS feeds or custom torrent sources you configure manually are queried on your behalf. The authors are not affiliated with these services and disclaim responsibility for their content or availability.
tos_section_7_b: You are the sole actor responsible for every download you initiate. The Software does not host, cache, or distribute any copyrighted material.
tos_section_8_b: These Terms may be updated at any time. Continued use of the Software following any update constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms.
tos_section_9_b:
FLUX does not collect or transmit any user data. All persisted data lives only on your device, inside the platform-specific application support directory:
  • Windows: %APPDATA%\flux-downloader\ (typically C:\Users\<you>\AppData\Roaming\flux-downloader\)
  • macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/flux-downloader/
  • Linux: ~/.config/flux-downloader/ (XDG_CONFIG_HOME default)
That folder contains: config.json (settings, named profiles, AcoustID key, radio favourites, RSS feed list), queue.json (the active download queue), history.json (completed downloads log), and flux.log (a local diagnostic log). No data is sent to remote servers except: (a) API requests to the third-party services listed in section 6 (only when you enable them or use related features), (b) anonymous binary downloads from public GitHub releases (yt-dlp, Chromaprint, hls.js), and (c) update checks to the GitHub release endpoint of this project if auto-update is enabled. No telemetry, analytics, or usage tracking of any kind.
tos_section_10_b:
FLUX is freely and publicly distributable software. It can be downloaded, installed, and run from any country in the world. Because copyright law, fair-use provisions, private-copying exceptions, broadcasting rights, and torrent regulations vary significantly between jurisdictions, you (the user) are solely responsible for ensuring that your use of every feature of this Software complies with the laws of the country in which you reside and/or operate the Software.

Examples of jurisdiction-specific differences include: (a) the existence and scope of a "personal copy" exception (art. 71-sexies Italian LdA, §53 German UrhG, Australian "format-shifting" exception, etc.); (b) whether the source must be a "legitimate" copy to qualify; (c) whether time-shifting / recording broadcasts is permitted; (d) whether torrent uploads ("seeding") are tolerated or actionable; (e) whether circumvention of basic protections beyond DRM is illegal; (f) collective-rights organisations and applicable levies. The authors, contributors, and distributors of FLUX make no representation that any specific feature is legal in your jurisdiction.

If you are unsure about the legal status of a particular use, consult a qualified lawyer in your country before proceeding.
tos_section_11_b:
The Spotify tab reads publicly accessible track metadata (titles, artist names) from open.spotify.com/embed/ pages — no authentication, no Spotify-protected audio. For each track, FLUX runs a YouTube search by title and downloads the resulting audio via yt-dlp. Spotify's DRM-protected audio is never accessed or decoded.

This feature sits in a grey legal area:
  • Reading public metadata: generally permitted but Spotify's ToS prohibits "scraping" — enforcement against personal use is non-existent in practice.
  • Downloading from YouTube: depends on (a) whether the specific upload is by the rights-holder (Vevo, label channel), (b) whether your jurisdiction permits private copying of broadcasts, (c) whether you have legitimate access to the underlying work (e.g. an active Spotify subscription or own a copy of the album).
By using this feature you confirm that: (i) you have legitimate access to the source content, (ii) the downloaded files are for personal non-commercial use only, (iii) you accept full responsibility under the copyright law of your country (see Section 10). If your jurisdiction does not recognise a personal-copy exception, or if you do not have legitimate access, do not use this feature.
tos_section_12_b:
FLUX is made possible by the open-source community and by creators whose assets are bundled with the app. Attributions required by the corresponding licences:

Audio — Sound Effect by Mauricio Póvoa from Pixabay
settings_subtitles_help: FLUX can find subtitles online for any local video via OpenSubtitles.com. Free registration required — get an API key from opensubtitles.com/consumers (free tier: 200 downloads/day). Without a key the "Find online" button stays disabled.
subs_search_help: Results are matched by file hash (most accurate) then by title. Click a row to download the .srt next to the video.
subs_search_empty: No results — type a title and search, or close and configure your OpenSubtitles API key in Settings.
subs_no_api_key: Configure your OpenSubtitles API key in Settings → Subtitles.
subs_no_results: No subtitles found. Try a different title or language.
subs_err_invalid_key: OpenSubtitles API key rejected. Check it in Settings.
subs_err_rate_limited: OpenSubtitles rate-limited you. Try again in a minute.
dedup_help: Acoustic fingerprint comparison (Chromaprint). Files with ≥85% similarity in the first ~6 seconds are grouped. The "best" file in each group (largest file size, proxy for highest bitrate) is pre-selected to keep; others are marked for deletion.
dedup_confirm_body: Move {n} file(s) to the OS trash? This can be undone from your file manager's trash.
dedup_help_name: Normalises filenames (strips extension, lowercases, removes punctuation and common tags like "(2)", "[copy]") and groups files that match. Instant. Catches accidental copies but misses different formats of the same song.
donate_intro: FLUX is free and open-source. Servers, code-signing, and the time spent maintaining it are paid out of pocket. If FLUX is useful to you, consider supporting development — every contribution helps keep the project independent and ad-free.
donate_github_sub: Recurring or one-off. 0% fees for OSS — every cent reaches the project.
donate_kofi_sub: "Buy me a coffee" — one-click one-off donation, no signup required.
donate_liberapay_sub: Recurring micro-donations (€1/week and up), built for FOSS, 0% fees.
donate_paypal_sub: Direct one-off donation via PayPal.me, any amount you choose.
donate_thanks: Thank you — your support keeps FLUX free, open, and growing.
donate_no_urls: No donation platforms have been configured yet. The maintainer can set them in Settings → Donations.
home_donate_body: FLUX is free and open-source. If it earns a place in your workflow, consider supporting its development — donations cover hosting, signing certificates, and the time spent maintaining the project.
video_sub: Bulk convert, resize, compress, extract audio and rename across a set of videos.
video_empty: Load videos to start. Operations apply to selected files (or all loaded files when none are selected).
video_convert_help: Re-encode to another container/codec. MP4 (H.264) is the most compatible.
video_resize_help: Scale down to a target height; aspect ratio preserved. Output is H.264 MP4.
video_compress_help: Shrink file size with H.264 (CRF). Stronger = smaller and lower quality.
video_extract_help: Pull the audio track out of each video into a separate file.
files_sub: Copy or sync a media folder to a device (USB, drive). Always preview before you run.
files_mode_incremental_desc: Copies only new or changed files to the destination.
files_copy_audio: , only audio files will be copied.
files_copy_video: , only video files will be copied.
files_mode_twoway_desc: Updates both folders — the newest version of each file wins.
files_mode_mirror_desc: Makes the destination an exact copy of the source — also deletes extra files there.
files_same_folder: Source and destination can't be the same folder.
files_need_paths: Pick a source and a destination first.
files_mirror_warn: Mirror mode will DELETE the listed files from the destination.
files_confirm_mirror_body: Mirror will permanently DELETE {n} file(s) on the destination that are not in the source. Continue?
files_identical: The two folders are already in sync.
files_src_set: Source set — pick a destination, then Preview.
files_transcode_hint: Convert non-MP3 audio (FLAC, Opus, M4A…) to MP3 during sync — car-friendly. Needs ffmpeg.
files_m3u_hint: Write a playlist.m3u on the destination (handy for car stereos) AND create an internal FLUX playlist from the synced folder's audio.
files_ren_confirm: Rename {n} file(s)? This changes the files on disk.
images_sub: Bulk rename, format conversion, resize, EXIF strip. Mass operations across a folder of images.
images_empty: Load images to start. Bulk operations apply to selected files (or all loaded files when none are selected).
images_convert_help: Convert to another format. JPG/WebP/AVIF accept a quality (1-100). PNG is lossless.
images_resize_help: Max width / max height (in px) or scale percentage. Aspect ratio preserved. Leave a field at 0 to ignore.
images_exif_help: Strip all metadata (privacy: removes GPS, camera info, date, etc.) or auto-rotate to the correct orientation per the existing EXIF Orientation tag.
images_heic_help: Convert iPhone .heic/.heif files to JPG. The originals are kept; converted copies are placed next to them.
images_overwrite: Overwrite originals (destructive — operations write to the source path)
xtract_image_crop_help: Drag a rectangle on the image to select the crop region. Use the aspect-ratio presets to constrain proportions. X/Y/W/H inputs stay in sync with the rectangle.
xtract_image_fx_help: Live preview on the crop image above. Sliders are non-destructive until you click Apply — at that point sharp produces a new file next to the source. Combine with crop in either order: each apply reads from the saved file.
images_watermark_help: Overlays bold text on every selected image. Configure font size, colour, opacity, position (9-point grid), and optional drop shadow.
images_compress_help: Binary-search the encoder quality until the output file fits the target size. Useful for "all photos under 500 KB for the blog" workflows.
images_dedup_help: Perceptual hash (dHash) comparison — catches the same image in different resolutions, formats, or with minor edits. Threshold is Hamming distance (0-64 bits); 8 is a sensible default.
images_similar_help: Groups visually-similar shots (bursts, near-duplicates) into a gallery and suggests the sharpest, highest-resolution one to keep. Sensitivity is the perceptual distance (0-64): higher groups looser sets, lower is stricter. Review, choose a keeper per group, then trash the rest.
similar_intro: Each group is a set of near-identical shots. The star badge marks the sharpest / highest-resolution one FLUX suggests keeping — click any photo to choose a different keeper. When you trash, every photo that is NOT the keeper is moved to the trash.
similar_none: No similar photos found across {n} images.
similar_found: Found {groups} group(s) of similar photos covering {files} files.
similar_nothing: Nothing to trash — every group keeps everything.
similar_confirm_body: Move {n} non-kept photo(s) to the trash? This can be undone from your file manager's trash.
images_organize_help: Move photos into dated subfolders by capture date (EXIF, with file date as fallback). Tokens: {year} {month} {day} {date} {make} {model} {camera} {ext}. Example: {year}/{month}. Files are moved.
images_organize_no_root: Set a destination root (or a download folder in Settings).
settings_image_library_pattern_help: Tokens: {year} {month} {day} {date} {make} {model} {camera} {ext}. Date from EXIF (file date fallback).
settings_image_library_root_help: Leave empty to use the default download folder.
images_timelapse_help: Combine the loaded images into a video. Each image becomes one frame; FPS controls how fast the sequence plays back. Order = list order. All images should ideally share the same resolution.
xtract_image_compare_help: Load a second image (usually the "after" version) and drag the divider to compare. Both images need the same dimensions for the comparison to be meaningful.
images_output_help: By default, processed files are saved in the default download folder set in Settings, into a /new subfolder. Customize below if you need a different destination.
settings_splash_audio: Play startup sound on splash screen
images_pdf_help: Bundle the loaded images into a single PDF, one image per page. Page size is A4 and each image is centred at its natural aspect — useful for delivering screenshot decks or photo contact sheets.
xtract_image_annotate_help: Cover text or other regions with shapes (use Eyedropper to sample the background colour), then add new text on top. Save flattens everything into a single PNG.
xtract_image_annotate_hint: Pick a tool, drag on the canvas. Click an object to select it for move / resize / delete.
notif_history_empty: No notifications yet in this session.
shortcuts_dnd_single: Auto-route to the right tab (audio / video / image / PDF)
shortcuts_dnd_multi: Image batch → Image Editor; Audio batch → Tag Editor
xtract_image_resize_help: Change the image dimensions in pixels (max W or H, keeping aspect ratio) or by percentage of the current size. Leave fields empty to skip.
media_drm_blocked_body: Netflix, Prime Video, Disney+, HBO Max and similar use DRM that FLUX cannot (and will not) bypass. Use the official app for these contents.
xtract_card_split_help: Detect track boundaries automatically (from chapter metadata or by silence between songs) and split the file into one track per row. Edit titles and remove unwanted segments before splitting.
xtract_image_recolor_help: Swap one colour for another across the whole image. Raise the tolerance to also catch nearby shades.
